methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $530 total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Nominal

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
MakinaRocks is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Sangmin Yoon that has raised 530 million KRW to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—alongside one public comparable, Palantir Technologies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $1.0B. Peer-median analysis across the five identified comparables yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. This wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the frontier AI subsector, where stage and capital raise alone do not fully explain valuation outcomes.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $856M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. This range reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression method and the significantly higher peer median, which is driven by elevated valuations in generalist AI development. Key uncertainties include the degree to which MakinaRocks' specific technical differentiation and market positioning align with the highest-valued peers versus the lower bound of the comparable set, as well as revenue or product traction data not present in this analysis.
